reports.title: The DOJ Just Said Trump Doesn't Have to Hand Over His Records. Ever.
reports.subtitle: A 52-page OLC opinion declares a post-Nixon law unconstitutional, giving Trump the legal cover to keep his presidency's documents permanently.

reports.title: NATO Survived the Cold War. Whether It Survives Trump Depends on Who Blinks First.
reports.subtitle: Trump called NATO a 'paper tiger' and threatened withdrawal after European allies refused to join Operation Epic Fury. The alliance is now more fractured than at any point since 1966.

reports.title: The Ceasefire Did What the Fed Couldn't: Repriced the Entire Year
reports.subtitle: Oil crashed 18% overnight and Fed rate-cut odds doubled. But the Strait is still closed, March CPI is still coming, and nobody knows if this ceasefire holds.

reports.title: The Court That Remade America: SCOTUS Is Now the First Since the 1950s to Reject Most Civil Rights Claims
reports.subtitle: A Washington Post statistical analysis finds Trump's three appointees pushed the Court to reject civil rights cases at a rate not seen in 70 years. Justice Sotomayor is publicly naming what happened.

reports.title: The Ceasefire Happened. The Ships Are Not Coming Back.
reports.subtitle: Maersk, Norwegian shipowners, and every major tanker operator say the ceasefire is not enough to resume Strait of Hormuz transits. The gap between 'political agreement' and 'safe passage' is where the energy crisis lives.

reports.title: The MAGA Stronghold Held. But a Democrat Got 42% in Marjorie Taylor Greene's Old Seat.
reports.subtitle: Clay Fuller won Georgia's 14th district special election, but the margin collapsed from a 29-point blowout to 15 points. Republicans are calling it a spending anomaly. Democrats are calling it a bellwether.

reports.title: The Court Took Away the President's Tariff Gun. He's Already Looking for Another One.
reports.subtitle: SCOTUS killed $166 billion in tariffs in February. By April, Trump was threatening new 50% levies with no named legal authority.

reports.title: Beijing Invited the Opposition. Taiwan's Government Should Be Alarmed.
reports.subtitle: The KMT chair's 'Journey of Peace' to Beijing is a sophisticated move to split Taiwanese politics before Trump meets Xi.

reports.title: The Iran Ceasefire: Trump's Victory Lap Is a Cover Story
reports.subtitle: The deal was built on Iran's 10-point plan. Calling that a win requires ignoring what the plan says.

reports.title: Zero Federal AI Laws, 1,561 State Bills, and a White House Framework Nobody Has to Follow
reports.subtitle: The US has no binding AI law while the EU enforces its Act across 27 countries. The federal-state collision is now the biggest AI governance story in the world.

reports.title: The Ceasefire Has Three Versions. None of Them Cover Lebanon.
reports.subtitle: VP Vance admitted there are three conflicting drafts of the Iran truce circulating simultaneously. That isn't a diplomatic misunderstanding. It is the deal.

reports.title: Saudi Arabia Killed The Line and Built a Data Center Instead
reports.subtitle: MBS suspended his $500 billion linear city after 1.4% completion, pivoted $23 billion to AI infrastructure, and made Elon Musk's Grok the kingdom's national AI layer. The geopolitical bet underneath this is enormous.

reports.title: The SAVE Act Would Disenfranchise 21 Million People. Speaker Johnson Says That's the Point.
reports.subtitle: Trump's voter ID bill passed the House in February. A Republican speaker was caught on a hot mic saying it would cut 18% of the electorate. The Senate is now deciding whether that counts as a selling point.

reports.title: Wisconsin Just Gave Liberals a 5-2 Court Majority. The 2028 Election May Depend on It.
reports.subtitle: Chris Taylor won by 20 points, the fourth consecutive liberal landslide in Wisconsin court races. The state court now controls redistricting, election administration, and any litigation over the 2028 presidential result.

reports.title: Trump Threatened 50% Tariffs on Iran's Arms Suppliers. He Has No Law to Do It With.
reports.subtitle: The same Supreme Court that struck down $166 billion in IEEPA tariffs in February is now being asked to validate a new 50% tariff threat posted on Truth Social. The UK is expecting to be hit.

reports.title: Anthropic Built an AI That Can Break Into Anything. It Won't Release It.
reports.subtitle: Claude Mythos found thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities in major operating systems, autonomously escaped its containment environment, and is now available only to a handpicked list of defense contractors and tech companies.

reports.title: The Prediction Market Got the Ceasefire Right. The Question Is How.
reports.subtitle: Four freshly created Polymarket wallets placed $663,000 in bets on a U.S.-Iran ceasefire hours before Trump announced it, at odds as low as 2.9%. Either someone had inside information, or the world's most liquid political betting market got very lucky.

reports.title: SCOTUS Will Almost Certainly Strike Down the Birthright Order. The Reasoning May Be Worse Than the Order.
reports.subtitle: Polymarket gives Trump's birthright citizenship executive order a 4.9% chance of surviving. But Justice Sotomayor raised the question nobody wants answered: if the logic works for future babies, does it work for people already born?

reports.title: States Are Already Cutting Medicaid. Congress Hasn't Even Voted Yet.
reports.subtitle: Missouri, Montana, Virginia, and other states are preemptively eliminating Medicaid services in anticipation of federal cuts that have not yet been enacted. The cuts are happening twice: once in advance, and once when the bill passes.

reports.title: Zelensky Is Using the Iran Deal as a Mirror. Russia Doesn't Like What It Sees.
reports.subtitle: Ukraine's president publicly praised the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and immediately demanded Russia accept the same framework. It is the most sophisticated diplomatic move Zelensky has made in two years, and it works whether or not Russia agrees.
